title: Design a resource and its operations
description: Model a new resource with consistent naming, standard methods, a paging contract for collections, and a defined error envelope.
body: Use when adding a resource to an API. Do not improvise field names or error shapes per resource. Actions: name the resource and its fields to match existing conventions; map create, read, update, delete, and list to standard methods; define the collection's paging contract; reuse the shared error envelope; capture it in the spec. Validation signals include a consumer predicting the shape from convention and the spec validating. Risk boundary: an inconsistent or unpaged resource is costly to change after clients adopt it, so align it with the surface before publishing.
